hero.title: Luma
hero.subtitle: How can we use screens — often seen as the enemy of early childhood — as an ally to counter their own harmful effects?

exploration.label: EXPLORATION
exploration.title: Starting from the field: social listening
exploration.intro: Before any hypothesis, we listened to parents on forums and social media to identify their real issues without recruitment bias.

method.description: Listening to conversations on forums and social media to identify parents' real concerns.

themes: Lack of support and concrete solutions
Socio-economic context (screens as a low-cost solution)
Addiction and compulsive behaviour in children
exploration.voteParagraph: The team voted with dot stickers to prioritise the most relevant themes to explore further.

stats.label: of 5 year olds use digital screens (INSEE)
average daily screen time for 3-6 year olds
of usage trajectories firmly set by age 2

framing.label: FRAMING
framing.title: Exploring causes with the 5 whys
framing.intro: For each prioritised theme, we used the 5 whys method to get to root causes. Example: Why do children spend too much time on screens?

steps: Children spend too much time on screens
Because it's a cheap, addictive form of entertainment
Because a child stays quiet in front of a screen (easy option for parents)
Because households are connected and leaving the child in front of the TV signs them up for content
Because parents lack time and resources
Because society values economic productivity over family time

causes: Spread / ease of access to screens
framing.commonPoint: Common to both profiles: magical thinking and screen exposure.

ideation.label: IDEATION
ideation.title: Generate and select solutions
ideation.intro: After framing, we ran an inspiration round table before generating ideas as a team.

tourDeTable.text: Each member shared inspiring references. Examples: Duolingo for gamification, interactive cartoons as an engaging format for children.

ideas.title: Interactive cartoon
ideas.description: Video content where the child interacts physically (jumping, blowing, shouting) with the story

ideas.title: App for parents
ideas.description: A collaborative app to create personalised activities based on the child's interests. Parents commit to making at least one activity available per month

ideas.title: Awareness app
ideas.description: An app for parents to understand the effects of screens and adapt their behaviour

vote.text: The team voted for the interactive cartoon, seen as more direct, more engaging for the child and more innovative.

workshop.label: DESIGN WORKSHOP
workshop.title: Define the product in 45-60 minutes
workshop.intro: Once the solution was chosen, we ran a structured workshop to define the product scope and identify potential barriers.

speedboat.description: The Speedboat helped us identify the project's driving forces (winds) and barriers (anchors). This helped the team anticipate obstacles before designing.

storyboard.description: We then storyboarded an example of interaction between Nova the star and a child, to make the user experience concrete before any mock-up.

affinityMapping.description: Generated ideas were grouped by theme to structure the product's features and objectives.

workshop.myRole: I contributed to all phases of the project. I took part in social listening, desk research, facilitating part of the workshops and creating personas. I notably took charge of Persona 3 — Margaux, an overwhelmed mother — and took part in storylining the final solution with Nova.

solution.label: SOLUTION
solution.title: Nova — The interactive cartoon
solution.intro: Our final solution: an interactive cartoon for 3-6 year olds. Nova, a little star, guides the child through adventures where they must interact physically — jumping, blowing, shouting — turning passive screen time into an active, sensory and educational experience.

exampleSteps.scenario: Nova arrives in a galaxy with colourful planets and meets Orion, a wise old comet.
Nova shows where she hurt herself after falling. To heal, the child must help.
exampleSteps.interaction: The child blows very hard to soothe the pain

exampleSteps.scenario: Mean meteors are approaching. Nova asks the child to jump on her back.
exampleSteps.interaction: The child jumps as high as they can
